channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: M23 rebels expand their control of Goma in eastern Congo
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels captured large parts of Goma, the biggest city in eastern Congo, including its airport, the United Nations said, as Rwanda's president on Wednesday joined calls for a ceasefire in the decadeslong conflict amid mounting international pressure.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International Criminal Court has no trial scheduled as Trump piles on pressure
item.description: The International Criminal Court has found itself without a single trial ahead for the first time in years.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 30 dead in stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India, police say
item.description: At least 30 people were killed and another 60 injured in a stampede at the Maha Kumbh festival, a six-week Hindu festival and the world’s largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 Palestinians killed by Israeli fire in occupied West Bank, officials say
item.description: Two Palestinians were killed by Israeli fire in the occupied West Bank overnight and into Wednesday, the Palestinian Health Ministry says. A 23-year-old man was shot dead in Tulkarem and a 25-year-old man was killed in a strike on Jenin, where Israel launched a large operation earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: India’s Modi speaks with 'dear friend' President Trump amid hopes of furthering ties
item.description: Following a call between President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, India is seeking to boost bilateral ties and trade with U.S. amid an already friendly relationship between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian police link stash of explosives and list of Jewish targets to an antisemitic plot
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian police believe explosives found on Sydney’s outskirts were evidence of a deadly escalation in a campaign of antisemitic arson and graffiti crimes that has been waged in major cities for months, officials said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 85 percent of Greenlanders don't want to be part of US: Survey
item.description: A large majority of residents in Greenland are against President Trump's wish to acquire the island territory, according to a new survey published Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Police say at least 30 people have died in a stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — At least 30 people were killed and many more injured in a stampede at the world’s largest religious gathering early Wednesday, police said, as millions of pilgrims rushed to dip in sacred waters during the Maha Kumbh festival in northern India.
item.dc:creator: RAJESH KUMAR SINGH and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Western officials warn Ukraine over weapons procurement dispute
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Western officials have warned Ukraine that an escalating rift between the defense minister and procurement chief may jeopardize trust in the country and urged the government to resolve the situation quickly so as not to disrupt weapons supplies.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US watchdog launches database on Catholic priests accused of sex abuse of minors in the Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A United States-based watchdog on Wednesday launched an online database on more than 80 Roman Catholic priests who have been accused of sexually abusing minors in the Philippines and said the silence of Filipino bishops on the crimes amounted to a cover-up.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl brings Arab and Israeli scientists together
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — It soars over the borders and divisions of the war-torn Middle East, bringing Arab and Israeli academics together. An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl is now getting global attention.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 14 members of an Australian religious group are convicted over diabetic girl's death
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Two bereaved parents and 12 fellow members of an Australian religious congregation accused of killing an 8-year-old girl by withholding her diabetes medication were found guilty on Wednesday of manslaughter. Elizabeth Rose Struhs died on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil to set up deportee reception center after contentious flight from US
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — The Brazilian government Tuesday said it will create a reception center for deported migrants from the United States following controversy over conditions on a recent deportation flight.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiger poachers use fishing boats to smuggle body parts out of Malaysia, study shows
item.description: Commercial fishing fleets have been playing a key role in the trafficking of parts of tigers poached in Malaysia, according to new research, information that could help enforcement efforts to save the critically endangered cat.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Migrants describe flights aboard US military planes carrying out Trump's swift deportations
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — Margarita Raymundo walked down the ramp of the U.S. Air Force cargo jet and onto the tarmac of Guatemala City’s airport, barely three days after a U.S. Border Patrol agent had apprehended her, along with three other migrants.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo tries to slow the assault of Rwanda-backed rebels amid reports of dead bodies in the streets
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Congolese security forces on Tuesday tried to slow the advance of Rwanda-backed M23 rebels who say they have captured Goma after entering eastern Congo's largest city, as U.N. officials reported violence, looting and bodies in the streets.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US ships return to Red Sea amid Gaza ceasefire
item.description: U.S. ships are returning to the Red Sea following promises from Yemen’s Houthi rebels to abstain from attacks on American and British vessels. The pledge, which comes after more than a year of shipping traffic disruption on a major international trade route, was made on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon's advocates fear Trump's return means little US help to protect rainforest
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Alexis Damancio Silva can’t forget the hardship in his town of Puerto Narino in far southern Colombia last year when extreme drought nearly dried up the Amazon River. Pink dolphins and fish died. Crops dried up. The town lost its easy access to markets.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Denmark increasing military spending amid Trump Greenland acquisition talk
item.description: Denmark this week announced a $2 billion security package for Greenland, making another large commitment to the defense of the Arctic nation as President Trump repeatedly calls to acquire the island.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: South African elephant kills tourist who was trying to save children at Kruger National Park
item.description: A tourist at South Africa's Kruger National Park was trampled to death by an elephant as he was trying to protect children, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU military chief says it would make sense to put European troops in Greenland, Welt reports
item.description: Top-ranking European military official Robert Brieger reportedly said it would "make perfect sense" to station troops in Greenland amid President Trump's interest in acquiring it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus' President Lukashenko extends rule after election rejected by opposition, EU
item.description: Belarus held an orchestrated election over the weekend that the opposition and the EU rejected as a farce, extending President Lukashenko's more than 30 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel, Hamas reach deal to release hostages and allow Palestinians to return to Gaza Strip
item.description: An agreement has been reached between Israel and Hamas that involves the release of additional hostages and the return of Palestinians to the northern part of the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian human rights lawyer, Nobel Prize winner urges Trump to show strength and end the war
item.description: Ukrainian human rights attorney and Nobel Peace Prize winner Oleksandra Matviichuk urges President Trump to show strength as he tries to bring the Russia-Ukraine war to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean prosecutors indict impeached president who declared martial law
item.description: Impeached South Korea President Yoon Suk Yeol was indicted on charges of leading an insurrection after briefly declaring martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's world leaders club: who’s in and who's looking to salvage ties
item.description: President Donald Trump's second term is already bringing massive changes as global leaders, allies and adversaries alike, watch to see where they fall in the pecking order and vie for a seat at the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio demands answers with 2 more Americans reportedly held by Taliban
item.description: Two Americans remain in Taliban custody after a two-for-one prisoner exchange the Biden administration negotiated before departing the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
